reasoning: Q6 is 1.75x slower than DuckDB target (33.8ms vs 19.3ms) with a clear bottleneck: full table scan without zone map block pruning accounts for 56% of execution time. The storage design includes zone maps for the three filtered columns, and query selectivity is low (1.9%), enabling significant I/O and CPU savings. This is the final iteration, so aggressive optimization is justified. Zone map pruning (low-risk, high-impact) should reduce total time by 30-50% to ~20-23ms, approaching DuckDB performance.
focus_areas: Load and use zone map indexes (shipdate, discount, quantity) to skip non-qualifying blocks before scanning
Implement AVX2 SIMD vectorization for predicate filtering to accelerate row evaluation on qualifying blocks
Reorder predicate evaluation to check most selective conditions first (discount=2/11 values, then shipdate, then quantity)
strategy_notes: Prioritize zone map block pruning as the primary optimization—the Learner analysis shows this can skip ~67% of blocks, reducing I/O volume from 60M to 20M rows. This addresses the dominant bottleneck directly. Layer SIMD vectorization on top to accelerate the predicate evaluation on the reduced working set. Predicate reordering is a low-risk, low-overhead tweak to minimize unnecessary comparisons. Combined, these three optimizations target both I/O reduction (zone maps) and CPU acceleration (SIMD + predicate order), addressing the dominant scan_filter operation comprehensively. Target final performance: 18-22ms, within 1.0-1.2x of DuckDB.
notes: This is the final iteration (remaining=0). Iteration 2 succeeded with correctness fixes, demonstrating the system handles multiple optimizations well. Zone maps are available in storage design and verified working. All three recommendations are low-to-medium risk with additive benefits. Avoid over-optimization: implement these three targeted techniques rather than attempting all six recommendations from the Learner analysis.
